title: Fantastic Fest

infobox.value: Active
[Film festival](/wiki/Film_festival "Film festival")
[Austin, Texas](/wiki/Austin,_Texas "Austin, Texas")
United States
September 18–25, 2025
[Alamo Drafthouse](/wiki/Alamo_Drafthouse_Cinema "Alamo Drafthouse Cinema")
[fantasticfest.com](http://fantasticfest.com)

is an annual [film festival](/wiki/Film_festival "Film festival") in [Austin, Texas](/wiki/Austin,_Texas "Austin, Texas"), focused on [fantasy](/wiki/Fantasy_film "Fantasy film"), [horror](/wiki/Horror_film "Horror film"), [science fiction](/wiki/Sci-fi_film "Sci-fi film"), [action](/wiki/Action_film "Action film") and [cult](/wiki/Cult_film "Cult film") films. It was founded in 2005 by [Tim League](/wiki/Tim_League "Tim League") of [Alamo Drafthouse](/wiki/Alamo_Drafthouse_Cinema "Alamo Drafthouse Cinema"). It is the largest and most prestigious genre festival in the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States"), and as one of the top three in the world alongside [Sitges](/wiki/Sitges_Film_Festival "Sitges Film Festival") and [Fantasia](/wiki/Fantasia_International_Film_Festival "Fantasia International Film Festival").[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Weinstein, Max (March 23, 2022). ["The Best Horror Festivals in the World 2022"](https://www.dreadcentral.com/news/424491/the-best-horror-festivals-in-the-world-2022/). *Dread Central*. Retrieved March 1, 2025.

Weinstein, Max (December 23, 2020). ["The Best Horror Festivals in the World 2021"](https://www.dreadcentral.com/news/364442/the-best-horror-festivals-in-the-world-2021/). *Dread Central*. Retrieved March 1, 2025.
["World's 50 Best Genre Film Festivals 2021 — MovieMaker"](https://www.moviemaker.com/best-genre-festivals-2021/3/). *www.moviemaker.com*. October 27, 2020. Retrieved March 6, 2025.

["Top 100 Fantastic & Horror Film Festivals in 2025 + Submission Tips"](https://adriantofei.com/top-100-international-horror-film-festivals/). *Adrian Țofei • Official Site*. January 28, 2015. Retrieved March 7, 2025.

["Lisa Dreyer named festival director at Fantastic Fest"](https://www.screendaily.com/news/lisa-dreyer-named-festival-director-at-fantastic-fest/5168054.article) [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20220519222303/https://www.screendaily.com/news/lisa-dreyer-named-festival-director-at-fantastic-fest/5168054.article) May 19, 2022, at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine"); in: Screen Daily, 24 February 2022

["Torches at Fantastic Fest: Sorting Through the Ashes of a Film Festival"](https://www.laweekly.com/film/fantastic-fest-may-be-2017s-most-controversial-film-festival-but-not-for-its-movies-8694952). *LA Weekly*. Retrieved January 27, 2019.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Annual film festival held in Austin, Texas, US

| Fantastic Fest | |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Status | Active |
| Genre | [Film festival](/wiki/Film_festival "Film festival") |
| Location | [Austin, Texas](/wiki/Austin,_Texas "Austin, Texas") |
| Country | United States |
| Inaugurated | 2005; 21 years ago (2005) |
| Most recent | September 18–25, 2025 |
| Organized by | [Alamo Drafthouse](/wiki/Alamo_Drafthouse_Cinema "Alamo Drafthouse Cinema") |
| Website | [fantasticfest.com](http://fantasticfest.com) |

**Fantastic Fest** is an annual [film festival](/wiki/Film_festival "Film festival") in [Austin, Texas](/wiki/Austin,_Texas "Austin, Texas"), focused on [fantasy](/wiki/Fantasy_film "Fantasy film"), [horror](/wiki/Horror_film "Horror film"), [science fiction](/wiki/Sci-fi_film "Sci-fi film"), [action](/wiki/Action_film "Action film") and [cult](/wiki/Cult_film "Cult film") films. It was founded in 2005 by [Tim League](/wiki/Tim_League "Tim League") of [Alamo Drafthouse](/wiki/Alamo_Drafthouse_Cinema "Alamo Drafthouse Cinema"). It is the largest and most prestigious genre festival in the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States"), and as one of the top three in the world alongside [Sitges](/wiki/Sitges_Film_Festival "Sitges Film Festival") and [Fantasia](/wiki/Fantasia_International_Film_Festival "Fantasia International Film Festival").[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Lisa Dreyer is festival director.[[5]](#cite_note-5) [Annick Mahnert](/wiki/Annick_Mahnert "Annick Mahnert") is head of programming.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fantastic_Fest&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The festival takes place in September at the Alamo Drafthouse South Lamar, filling eight screens for eight days and hosting many writers, directors, and actors, either well-established or emergin. The festival has become known as a launch-pad for genre films, where critical acclaim at the fest can lead to big box office returns.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

A notable feature of this festival is the inclusion of "secret screenings". For these screenings, the audience often does not know what the film will be until seated, moments before it begins.[[7]](#cite_note-7) It also features many themed parties, outings, film-themed "feasts", and other events that are hallmarks of the original [Alamo Drafthouse Cinema](/wiki/Alamo_Drafthouse_Cinema "Alamo Drafthouse Cinema").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b2/Wachowskis%2C_Fantastic_Fest%2C_Cloud_Atlas.jpg/250px-Wachowskis%2C_Fantastic_Fest%2C_Cloud_Atlas.jpg)](/wiki/File:Wachowskis,_Fantastic_Fest,_Cloud_Atlas.jpg)

[The Wachowskis](/wiki/The_Wachowskis "The Wachowskis"), Fantastic Fest 2012

In 2007, *[Variety](/wiki/Variety_(magazine) "Variety (magazine)")* publisher Charles Koones included Fantastic Fest as one of "Ten Festivals We Love".[[8]](#cite_note-8) In 2008, *[MovieMaker](/wiki/MovieMaker "MovieMaker")* named it one of "The 25 Film Festivals Worth the Entry Fee".[[9]](#cite_note-9) In 2017, *[MovieMaker](/wiki/MovieMaker "MovieMaker")* included the festival on its list of "The 25 Coolest Film Festivals in the World."[[10]](#cite_note-10)

In 2022, an online-exclusive section of programming was created called "Burnt Ends", which was said to showcase "the weirdest, wildest, most fringe films out there."[[11]](#cite_note-11)
